definition.category: Kolm is the verifiable AI compiler and artifact contract layer: traces and specs become portable signed artifacts with eval gates, receipts, runtime targets, and governance.
definition.spine: evidence -> dataset -> eval -> build decision -> artifact -> runtime -> receipt -> governance export

dimensions.label: Model provider
dimensions.required_affordance: Every inference and teacher path must let the user choose or override the provider/model.

dimensions.label: Compute target
dimensions.required_affordance: Training, distill, quantize, and runtime flows must expose local, remote, and hosted compute instead of assuming local hardware.

dimensions.label: Artifact runtime
dimensions.required_affordance: Compiled artifacts must record target runtime, quantization, model hash, and verifier metadata.

dimensions.label: Storage plane
dimensions.required_affordance: Artifacts, lake exports, and compliance bundles must be movable between local, cloud, and air-gapped storage.

dimensions.label: Privacy mode
dimensions.required_affordance: Every capture and lake route must tell the user what is stored, what is redacted, and how to turn storage off.

dimensions.label: Deployment mode
dimensions.required_affordance: The user must be able to run the same artifact in hosted, local, enterprise, and agent contexts.

dimensions.label: Governance mode
dimensions.required_affordance: Enterprise controls must be explicit account surfaces, not hidden configuration folklore.

dimensions.label: Proof mode
dimensions.required_affordance: Every shipped model claim must have a verifier, replay, or benchmark path the user can run themselves.

journeys.name: Gateway Capture

next_actions.label: Connect a provider or point an SDK at the Kolm base URL.
Run `kolm capture --provider openai --as local --json` or open account connectors.

journeys.user_story: As a developer, I can replace my OpenAI, Claude, OpenRouter, Gemini-compatible, or self-hosted base URL and start capturing only the calls I choose.

journeys.name: Privacy Lake

next_actions.label: Filter the lake by provider, model, status, namespace, and latency.
Capture sample calls or import a JSONL corpus.

journeys.user_story: As a regulated operator, I can inspect what was captured, redact sensitive values, export rows, and switch to zero-retention or differential-privacy aggregates.

journeys.name: Datasets And Labeling

next_actions.label: Approve the next highest-value example or create a dataset from repeated work.
Run `kolm demo seed-log-triage` or connect a provider to create candidates.

journeys.user_story: As a model builder, I can convert repeated work into labeled train/eval sets with review queues, simulations, and holdout splits.

journeys.name: Train And Distill

next_actions.label: Choose teacher, student, compute backend, eval gate, and output target before starting a build.
Create a dataset, run `kolm distill strategy --json`, or run `kolm bench evidence --summary` before making benchmark claims.

journeys.user_story: As a user without perfect local hardware, I can train or distill via local compute, remote SSH GPU, managed GPU, Together, OpenAI/Claude teachers, or BYOC.

happy_path: bind to compile or distill spec
journeys.name: Models And Backbones

next_actions.label: Pick the best backbone for the task and target device before starting a distill or compile.
Run `kolm models recommend --json` or open the model catalog.

journeys.user_story: As a builder, I can choose real backbones such as Gemma 3, Gemma 3n, MedGemma, EmbeddingGemma, Qwen, Phi, Llama, Mistral, and SmolLM by task, license, modality, memory, and target device.

journeys.name: Multimodal Tokenization

next_actions.label: Tokenize media into sidecars, then review/bake off the generated examples before training.
Run `kolm media tokenize --path <file> --json` on a local file.

journeys.user_story: As a multimodal user, I can tokenize images, audio, video, PDFs, text, and code into searchable Markdown sidecars with local feature tokens and optional captions or transcripts.

journeys.name: Compile And Verify

next_actions.label: Compile, choose quantization/runtime strategy, verify, and export an artifact with receipts.
Run `kolm build <name>` to create a first artifact.

journeys.user_story: As an operator, I can compile a task into a signed .kolm artifact, verify it offline, diff versions, and export runtime-specific assets.

journeys.name: Runtime Inference

next_actions.label: Select artifact, fallback chain, runtime target, and device class.
Open the artifact list or use `kolm chat-tui --model=openai:gpt-5` to compare hosted versus compiled output.

journeys.user_story: As an app developer, I can run a compiled artifact locally, in the browser, on edge/server runtimes, or behind OpenAI-compatible fallback chains.

journeys.name: Compute And Cloud

next_actions.label: Run the cloud broker and readiness doctors to choose the cheapest compliant compute, storage, and deployment profile before launching work.
Configure R2/S3/Supabase and a GPU provider env var, or add remote SSH.

journeys.user_story: As a team with no GPU, I can configure R2-compatible object storage, S3-compatible storage, AWS, Supabase, Modal, RunPod, Lambda, Together, or remote SSH and still build artifacts.

journeys.name: Devices And Fleet

next_actions.label: Detect hardware, recommend runtime targets, create team tunnels when needed, and install verified artifacts.
Run `kolm devices detect --json` from the target machine.

journeys.user_story: As an edge or enterprise user, I can detect devices, choose compatible runtimes, create team reverse tunnels, test installability, and push artifacts to local, SSH, HTTP, or air-gapped targets.

journeys.name: Enterprise Governance

next_actions.label: Review access, cost, audit, and compliance state before scaling usage.
Create scoped API keys and export the compliance package.

journeys.user_story: As an enterprise admin, I can control tenants, scoped keys, approvals, audit logs, billing, privacy, compliance export, and SSO/SCIM-ready identity.

journeys.name: Agents And Registry

next_actions.label: Install the verified artifact into the user agent harness or registry.
Compile with `--as-mcp` or run `kolm serve --mcp` locally.

journeys.user_story: As an agent-platform user, I can compile artifacts as MCP tools, install them into Claude/Cursor/Continue/Cline, publish or pull registry artifacts, and replay hashed run logs.

failure_codes.next: Use a scoped key with the required permission or ask an admin.
Upgrade or choose a local mode.
Connect a provider key or choose a local runtime.
Retry, switch provider, or use fallback policy.
Use local mode, redaction, or policy approval.
Capture, label, import, or synthesize more examples.
Create or import an eval suite.
Inspect failing cases, add labels, or change model.
Configure local GPU, hosted GPU, BYOC, or remote SSH.
Configure R2, S3, Supabase, or local durable storage.
Reject the artifact and rebuild or re-download.
Export a compatible target or choose another runtime.
Choose an allowed provider/runtime or update policy.
Check live certification status before marketing the claim.

requirements.title: Publish version-pinned .kolm v1/RS-1 format specification with ZIP members and compatibility rules.
Offline verifier can validate artifact signatures without hosted Kolm.
Submit or steward the .kolm format through a neutral standards/foundation process.
Get third-party runtime support from Hugging Face, Ollama, llama.cpp, ONNX/GGUF tooling, and hardware vendors.
K-score is computed per artifact, gates composite and axes, and is backed by a frozen public 30-case calibration suite.
Train/eval split integrity is locked by row hashes, holdout gates, and production-ready checks.
Recompile against new captured data without rebuilding the whole namespace.
When K-score or gates fail, CLI/API prints failing cases, gate reasons, and next actions.
Distillation planner chooses collect/rule/cache/SFT/KD/rejection/preference/on-policy/spec-decode paths and refuses fake teacher plans.
Hosted compile is async, returns job ids, and supports deploy/webhook notification hooks.
Repeated compile/runtime work uses deterministic cache keys where safe.
Compiler binds exported GGUF/ONNX/Safetensors/CoreML/MLX/ExecuTorch/TensorRT targets with provenance.
OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic-native gateway/capture paths both exist and are not OpenAI-only.
Per-request zero-retention/no-store mode forwards calls without persisting capture/event rows.
Operators can reduce lake noise by filtering captured calls by provider/model/status/latency/namespace.
Local event lake has canonical schema, spend, latency, redaction, provider, and exportable rows.
PII/PHI redaction is fail-closed, class-counted, and backed by a public synthetic precision/recall/F1 benchmark.
Lake aggregates can be returned with deterministic Laplace differential privacy noise.
OTLP/OpenTelemetry hooks exist for request/artifact telemetry.
Team capture, tenant fencing, and role/key scope controls are represented across auth, account, and audit surfaces.
Signed .kolm artifacts run locally and expose receipts through CLI/API/MCP.
Browser web runtime is packaged for embedding.
Edge/serverless runtime story exists for Cloudflare/Vercel/Deno-style deployments.
iOS, Android, and React Native SDK source exists; package publication remains external.
Local compute matrix covers OpenVINO and Qualcomm QNN/Hexagon in addition to CPU/CUDA/MPS/ROCm/DirectML.
Constraint-first broker chooses local, SSH, rented GPU, managed training, customer cloud, or edge runtime without leaking secrets or launching spend.
Runtime telemetry can be emitted through local logs and OTEL when explicitly enabled.
Runtime compatibility and LTS policy is documented for v1 artifacts.
Registry/marketplace exposes artifact search/discovery by category, license, verified state, and K-score.
Artifact/version diff and rollback surfaces exist for governance.
One-click deploy plans and button metadata exist for BYOC, edge, SSH, GPU, and managed training targets.
Registry supports publisher badge policy, verification evaluation, and artifact-level readiness metadata.
Private tenant registry/account artifact inventory exists; air-gapped/private registry productization needs deployment proof.
Artifact dependency graph and blast-radius inspection are generated from manifest/provenance metadata.
Artifact signing, receipts, verification, rotation, auditor attestations, and Sigstore hooks exist.
Provider/model routing exists across OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Gemini, local, and compute backends.
Benchmark harness and hardware/model catalog exist; public reproducible leaderboard data needs publication.
Replay, bakeoff, trace, and eval surfaces can compare candidates before promotion.
Spend attribution by tenant/namespace/provider/model/billing period exists.
Per-tenant rate limiting and quota enforcement are in the auth middleware.
Compile/deploy hooks, billing webhook handling, and event/audit surfaces exist.
Node, Python, MCP, VS Code, C, Rust, TypeScript package, Swift, Kotlin, and React Native SDK surfaces exist locally.
API keys are scoped/hashed, local BYOK secrets are encrypted, and external Vault/1Password/AWS/GCP/Azure refs stay out of artifacts.
SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA BAA, GDPR, FedRAMP, and formal SLSA/SBOM evidence require live certification/auditor process.
Deterministic prompt compression is wired into runtime policy while public performance claims remain benchmark-scoped.
Exact and semantic runtime caches are wired into gateway decisions with TTL, threshold, and policy controls.
Fallback/provider chains exist in connectors, model routing, and runtime copy; explicit per-artifact policy needs tighter enforcement.
K-score, bakeoff, eval, production-ready verdicts, and per-call quality judge calibration score artifact quality.
RAG/index sidecars are available to artifact recipes through the runtime; dense/rerank variants remain dependency-gated.
Streaming/SSE support has a provider parity contract, normalizer, capture live tail, and route-level capability endpoint.
Runtime policy and artifact execution enforce explicit token/input budgets before model calls or recipe execution.
A/B bakeoff, replay, trace verification, evals, and statistical comparison surfaces exist.
CLI exposes the full product loop with compile, train, distill, verify, run, TUI, doctor, cache, models, compute, MCP, billing, and deploy paths.
`kolm compile --as-mcp` creates project-local MCP config and skill sidecars for agents.
Account UI covers the gateway, capture, lake, privacy, repeated workflows, opportunities, labels, datasets, simulations, bakeoffs, builds, artifacts, devices, storage, billing, and settings surfaces.
`kolm doctor` diagnoses environment, keys, toolchain, project, and K-score issues.
Install scripts exist; public package-manager channels need release-backed publication.

journey_research.why_it_matters: Modern distillation products combine captured completions, evals, and fine-tuning; Kolm must add provider-independent artifacts and local/runtime proof.
Claude is a native provider surface, not an afterthought behind OpenAI-compatible routing.
Gateway table stakes now include analytics, logging, caching, rate limiting, retries, and fallback.
Unified APIs, budgets, usage monitoring, load balancing, and fallbacks are baseline UX for AI gateways.
Production AI observability should use common GenAI spans, events, metrics, and provider attributes where possible.
Self-hosted serving engines increasingly expose OpenAI-compatible APIs; Kolm should route to them and compile away repeated work.
Portable inference has to target established runtimes and execution providers, not a proprietary-only runtime.
Edge/mobile deployment needs device-aware export targets for constrained hardware.
Enterprise buyers expect SSO, SCIM, audit logs, admin workflows, authorization, and secure vault patterns.

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Provider-hosted fine-tuning can create adapted models but remains provider-bound; Kolm must win on portability, receipts, local execution, and cross-runtime packaging.
surface_research.title: OpenAI supervised fine-tuning

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Provider caching reduces repeated prompt cost; Kolm must prove avoided calls and deterministic local hot paths, not just cache discounts.
surface_research.title: OpenAI prompt caching

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Prompt-cache block control is now standard for long-context apps; Kolm routing should preserve provider cache fallbacks while compiling stable repeated tasks.
surface_research.title: Anthropic prompt caching

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Datasets, experiments, traces, and online evaluators are table stakes; Kolm should import these signals and bind them into K-score evidence.
surface_research.title: LangSmith evaluation concepts

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Trace every call, step, and decision; Kolm receipts and trace export must interoperate with this workflow.
surface_research.title: LangSmith observability

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: OpenTelemetry-based traces and eval integrations are standard; Kolm should emit and ingest OpenTelemetry/OpenInference-compatible traces.
surface_research.title: Arize Phoenix

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Tracing, evaluation, and improvement loops are bundled in modern LLM platforms; Kolm must bind improvements to portable artifacts.
surface_research.title: W&B Weave

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: LoRA fine-tuning and multi-adapter serving are mature; Kolm should treat training backends as pluggable while owning artifact proof and runtime packaging.
surface_research.title: Predibase fine-tuning overview

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Managed LoRA and full fine-tuning are widely available; Kolm should not compete on generic training UX alone.
surface_research.title: Together AI fine-tuning

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Trace-to-fine-tune workflows are an adjacent wedge; Kolm must make trace imports and eval gates first-class.
surface_research.title: OpenPipe fine-tuning quick start

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Unified model access, routing, provider controls, and cost visibility are now a user expectation; Kolm's proxy must collect the same telemetry while preserving customer-owned provider keys and compiling repeated work into local artifacts.
surface_research.title: OpenRouter API and model routing docs

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: OpenAI-compatible proxy, spend tracking, fallbacks, and logging integrations are table stakes; Kolm must go beyond gateway mechanics by turning traces into signed local artifacts.
surface_research.title: LiteLLM Gateway

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Production AI gateways ship cache, retries, fallbacks, load balancing, budget limits, guardrails, and multimodal routing; Kolm must keep these compatible while adding private compile and verification.
surface_research.title: Portkey AI Gateway

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: LLM platforms now expose full traces, sessions, datasets, experiments, and online evaluations; Kolm's lake and opportunity engine must preserve trace fidelity and emit portable evidence.
surface_research.title: Langfuse observability overview

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: One-line proxy observability is a commodity surface; Kolm's capture path must remain drop-in while adding local-first ownership and compile-ready datasets.
surface_research.title: Helicone AI gateway and observability

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Serving thousands of LoRA adapters on one GPU is mature infrastructure; Kolm should treat multi-LoRA serving as a downstream target, not the unique moat.
surface_research.title: Predibase LoRAX multi-LoRA server

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Cloud training platforms now support imported LoRAs, live merge, and multi-LoRA deployment; Kolm must preserve export provenance and verify artifacts across those hosted runtimes.
surface_research.title: Fireworks LoRA deployment docs

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Small-model training and distillation are a competitive category, not a unique feature; Kolm should win on data capture, receipts, artifact portability, and local deployment.
surface_research.title: Arcee Small Language Models

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Phone-class multimodal backbones raise the minimum edge story; Kolm must recommend Gemma 3n where device budgets fit and record runtime_target/device_fit evidence.
surface_research.title: Gemma 3n model overview

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Managed distillation and fine-tuning are cloud-native enterprise expectations; Kolm should support BYOC/backend adapters and stronger artifact governance.
surface_research.title: Amazon Bedrock model customization

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: SSO, SCIM, admin portal, and audit logs are expected for enterprise identity; Kolm account/team surfaces must converge there.
surface_research.title: WorkOS docs

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: B2B org modeling is a product primitive; Kolm must separate org, workspace, user, and tenant semantics.
surface_research.title: Auth0 Organizations

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Self-serve subscription, invoice, and customer portal flows are table stakes; Kolm must move beyond payment-link-only certification.
surface_research.title: Stripe Billing

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Customer-managed billing is expected; Kolm account billing should expose portal and webhook-backed entitlement state.
surface_research.title: Stripe customer portal

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: On-device runtime targets are platform-native; Kolm should emit/verifiably wrap runtime-specific assets instead of pretending to replace them.
surface_research.title: Apple Core ML

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: On-device foundation model APIs raise the offline bar; Kolm needs proof, evals, receipts, and governance above native model access.
surface_research.title: Apple Foundation Models

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Android and edge deployment should target established runtimes; Kolm should be compiler/governance layer.
surface_research.title: Google LiteRT

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Cross-platform mobile inference requires model/runtime optimization; Kolm artifact manifests must name target runtime and proof.
surface_research.title: ONNX Runtime Mobile

surface_research.state_of_art_signal: Edge PyTorch deployment is a real substrate; Kolm should target it as an export backend.
surface_research.title: PyTorch ExecuTorch

route_surfaces.name: Identity, Access, Teams, And Billing
route_surfaces.promise: A buyer can sign up, authenticate, manage organizations and keys, change plans, and prove entitlement state without support intervention.

route_surfaces.name: Public Site, Docs, API Reference, And SDK Distribution
route_surfaces.promise: Every public page, API reference, SDK asset, and route contract is current, crawlable, coherent, and hash-verifiable.

route_surfaces.name: Compile, Artifact, Registry, Receipts, And Verification
route_surfaces.promise: A task can become a signed, portable, content-addressed artifact with K-score, receipt chain, registry metadata, and offline verification.

route_surfaces.name: Runtime, Inference, Connectors, And Multimodal APIs
route_surfaces.promise: Kolm can sit in the hot path as an OpenAI/Anthropic-compatible runtime, local artifact runner, and connector gateway with receipts and fallback behavior.

route_surfaces.name: Capture, Datasets, Evals, Labels, Training, And Improvement Loop
route_surfaces.promise: Production traces and user data can become governed datasets, labels, evals, simulations, distill runs, and safer follow-up artifacts.

route_surfaces.name: Governance, Compliance, Admin, Audit, Privacy, Trace, And Notifications
route_surfaces.promise: Operators can prove who did what, what data moved, what artifact ran, what privacy controls applied, and what compliance package can be exported.

route_surfaces.name: Deployment, Edge Devices, BYOC, Storage, Sync, Tunnel, And Federated Learning
route_surfaces.promise: Artifacts can move across cloud, BYOC, device, tunnel, sync, confidential-compute, and federated workflows without losing tenant boundaries or evidence.
